title: Feature Forge Epic Manifest
description: Canonical record of an epic's membership, dependency edges, charters, and contracts. Lives at {specsDir}/{epic}/epic-manifest.json. Carries NO per-feature status field (REQ-STATE-02); status is derived live from each member's .pipeline-state.json.

epic.description: Matches the epic subtree directory name. Globally unique, kebab-case.

narrativeDoc.description: Relative pointer to the narrative document (REQ-EPIC-03).

updatedAt.description: Bumped by every mutator on each atomic write (REQ-OBS-01).

features.description: Ordered. Order is the user-declared sequence; it is NOT a dependency ordering.

name.description: Globally unique across the whole specs tree, kebab-case (REQ-DIR-04).

charter.description: One-paragraph scope statement + contract obligations (REQ-EPIC-04). No full PRD at creation.

dependsOn.description: Names of sibling features in this epic (REQ-EPIC-02). Every entry must be a name in features[]. May be empty.

exposes.description: What this feature provides to dependents (REQ-EPIC-03). May be empty.

consumes.description: What this feature relies on from its dependencies (REQ-EPIC-03). May be empty.

mutatesShared.description: OPTIONAL precision hint for cross-member coupling detection (#144): project-root-relative file/glob paths this feature writes or migrates that are shared across the epic (e.g. a corpus a sibling's tests pin). When present, forge-verify CHECK-E10 uses it directly; when absent, CHECK-E10 falls back to grepping specs/backlog for mutated paths. Not a dependency edge — declarative only. May be omitted entirely.

name.description: Identifier of the exposed artifact (e.g. verifyJwt, JWT_SECRET).

from.description: Name of the sibling feature providing this. Must be present in features[].

name.description: Identifier being consumed; should match an exposes[].name of the from feature.
